localClaudeConventionInputs.usedFor: Claude agent Markdown locations, required name and description frontmatter, and optional tools allowlists.
Claude skill directory shape, SKILL.md frontmatter, and references/ progressive-disclosure pattern.
CLAUDE.md instructions, settings.json categories, and the boundary that secrets live outside tracked config.
Local agent frontmatter shape and concise role-body conventions.

localClaudeConventionInputs.inspection: Read one small declarative skill sample only.
localClaudeConventionInputs.usedFor: Local skill frontmatter and Use when trigger conventions.

localClaudeConventionInputs.inspection: Path/category-only listing; no private Runtime contents opened.
localClaudeConventionInputs.usedFor: Declarative candidate discovery for tracked templates and future symlink targets.

localClaudeConventionInputs.inspection: Path/category-only listing; contents intentionally not opened.
localClaudeConventionInputs.usedFor: Local-only runtime, cache, session, history, daemon, auth-adjacent, and local override classification.

repositoryWorkflowNucleus.summary: Use the OMP workflow-kit repo operating model as the harness nucleus workflow. Translate it into Claude-native instructions, settings, agents, and skills instead of copying live OMP or Claude runtime state.
portablePolicy: Keep a global layer for default workflow instructions, sticky safety rules, and reusable skills.
Keep a downstream project layer for repo-local instructions, repo config, optional docs/agents guidance when present, scope ledger, handoffs, issue/PR templates, labels, and project skills.
Use an idempotent apply/check model where a marker manifest defines what applied means and existing files are not overwritten.
Preserve traceability from grilled plan to PRD, issues, triage, one issue/worktree/PR implementation, verification evidence, and handoff when blocked.
Require project skills to live one directory below nucleus/skills with SKILL.md frontmatter and concrete Use when triggers.
Treat GitHub issue/PR templates and workflow labels as repo workflow surfaces when the repo uses GitHub.
claudeTranslation: Project-specific workflow policy belongs in a repo CLAUDE.md bridge plus repo-local nucleus/skills where it is useful across harnesses.
General reusable workflow policy belongs in user-level instruction or skill surfaces such as ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and ~/.agents/skills.
Claude agents are role adapters, not replacements for the workflow-kit issue lifecycle.
Future renderers must produce dry-run manifests before writing any live ~/.claude file.

declarativeCandidateSurfaces.notes: Thin Claude-native bridge to shared repo workflow instructions; no duplicated live runtime state.
Shareable deny rules and non-secret options only. Auth, env, MCP, model, and local override keys stay out of tracked templates.
Adapted OMP roles become Claude Markdown agents with local frontmatter conventions and read-heavy tool allowlists.
Dry-run examples only. Shared workflow source remains nucleus/skills or ~/.agents/skills until a future reviewed apply issue.
Curated per-skill symlinks from shared nucleus/skills roots. Never symlink the whole ~/.claude directory or whole skill roots.

templateBoundaries.boundary: Instruction bridges may import or point at shared repo workflow instructions. They must not paste secrets, machine-local paths, session history, or runtime state.
Tracked settings may carry shareable, non-secret keys only. They must not set credential helpers, auth refresh scripts, env secrets, MCP servers, default model, local memory paths, or hooks.

Agent Markdown files may define name, description, and a narrow tool allowlist. They must not embed credentials, provider routing, MCP server config, live machine paths, or write-capable tools in read-heavy review roles.
Dry-run skill templates may show adapter shape, but shared workflow source stays separate and future apply must not delete, overwrite, or dedupe existing Claude skills.
The skill symlink manifest may declare curated per-skill symlinks only. It must never copy, vendor, delete, or whole-root link existing skills.

ompAgentMappings.rationale: Preserve the UI/UX review role as a narrow Claude agent with read-heavy tools and no file edits.
Claude does not need OMP runtime semantics, but a read-only codebase scout is a useful role adapter.
Source-verified library/API research is distinct enough to preserve, while live source-fetching policy stays with the parent environment.
Low-reasoning mechanical worker overlaps Claude's native task delegation; no custom role file is needed.
Complex architectural planning benefits from a focused planning role when the main task needs sequencing and risk analysis.
Specialized test-authoring role with high-signal, contract-defending test discipline; worth preserving as a focused Claude agent.
Review discipline, security focus, and concise findings can be preserved as a read-only Claude review agent.
General-purpose delegated execution is already native Claude behavior; a direct OMP port would duplicate it.

skillCandidateMappings.rationale: Port bounded side-question discipline, not OMP ephemeral runtime subthreads.
Port the objective interview workflow; persistent goal state remains host-native.
Port handoff-writing discipline, not OMP session spawning.
Port complaint-to-rule drafting as a reviewable workflow; do not install generated rules automatically.
Port plan-before-execute guidance when a dedicated planning agent is too heavy.
Port tangential-work delegation prompts; do not claim to launch OMP background agents.

duplicateSkillRootRisks.risk: Reusable workflow skills may be shared across OMP, Codex, and Claude, but unversioned edits can drift from repo nucleus/skills.
duplicateSkillRootRisks.issue42Action: document-only; prefer curated per-skill symlink candidates; forbid-bulk-symlink

duplicateSkillRootRisks.risk: Codex-specific copies can diverge from the shared root and from Claude copies.
duplicateSkillRootRisks.issue42Action: document-only; do not mirror into Claude; reconcile in the later skill-root audit

duplicateSkillRootRisks.risk: Existing duplicated skills can be shadowed by new names or symlinks.
duplicateSkillRootRisks.issue42Action: document-only; do not delete, move, disable, rewrite, or dedupe existing skills

duplicateSkillRootRisks.risk: Repo-local project skills can collide by name with personal/global skills at different scopes.
duplicateSkillRootRisks.issue42Action: document-only; keep as project-scoped source of truth for future Claude symlink candidates

duplicateSkillRootRisks.risk: Copying workflow-kit content directly would entangle live OMP workflow state with Claude adapters.
duplicateSkillRootRisks.issue42Action: document-only; translate policy, do not copy

localOnlyClaudeSurfaces.reason: OAuth session, user/local MCP config, per-project trust/allowed-tools state, and local caches.
Per-project session transcripts and private conversation history.

generatedCandidateSurfaces.notes: Project instruction bridge candidate; render into a temp directory for review.
User instruction bridge candidate; future user-scope apply requires explicit approval.
Project settings candidate with shareable deny rules only.
User settings candidate; live merge is deferred and must preserve existing local settings.
User-scoped reusable adapted OMP role agents.
Project skill adapter examples only; future apply must use curated per-skill review.
User skill adapter examples only; future apply must use curated per-skill review.
Candidate manifest for reviewed per-skill symlinks from shared roots; no bulk root action.

dryRunValidationStrategy: Render templates into a temporary directory, never directly into ~/.claude or project .claude.
Parse every rendered JSON template with JSON.parse and every agent or skill Markdown template's YAML frontmatter.
Compare OMP agent mapping rows against distributions/snapshots/omp-builtins/source.json and fail when a bundled agent is unmapped or duplicated.
Compare OMP skill candidate rows against distributions/snapshots/omp-builtins/portability-matrix.json and require sharedWorkflowSource to stay distinct from generatedClaudeAdapter templates.
Validate that the Claude adapter preserves the workflow-kit repo lifecycle as portable policy rather than copying live OMP or Claude runtime files.
Reject rendered files containing absolute private home paths, API key/token-looking text, credential-cache destinations, MCP/provider/auth keys, hooks, env secrets, or a default model change.
Validate that the instruction bridge imports @AGENTS.md and that the skill symlink template uses curated per-skill candidates only.
Print a dry-run manifest of candidate destination paths, required human approvals, and skipped local-only surfaces before any live ~/.claude modification is allowed.
Require a separate future issue/PR before writing to live ~/.claude, project .claude, or shared nucleus/skills.
humanDecisionsBeforeImplementation: Which adapted OMP roles should become project-scoped .claude/agents versus user-scoped ~/.claude/agents?
Should adapted Claude agents pin a model if local Claude docs later expose a stable model field, or should they inherit the parent session model?
Which skill candidates should be repo-local nucleus/skills versus personal ~/.agents/skills, and how should Claude consume them?
Should Claude instructions duplicate AGENTS.md or bridge to it?
What review/approval policy is required before writing any generated template into live ~/.claude?

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Which adapted OMP roles should become project-scoped .claude/agents versus user-scoped ~/.claude/agents?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Project-specific roles should be project-scoped under .claude/agents; general reusable roles should be user-scoped under ~/.claude/agents.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Should adapted Claude agents pin a model if local Claude docs later expose a stable model field, or should they inherit the parent session model?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Inherit the parent session model in this slice because the local Claude authoring docs inspected here establish name, description, and tools conventions without requiring a model field.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Which skill candidates should be repo-local nucleus/skills versus personal ~/.agents/skills, and how should Claude consume them?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Use the workflow-kit split: repo workflow and domain skills go in nucleus/skills; general reusable workflow skills go in ~/.agents/skills; Claude consumes them via curated per-skill symlinks or reviewed adapter templates, never whole-root copies.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: Should Claude instructions duplicate AGENTS.md or bridge to it?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Bridge: a project CLAUDE.md imports @AGENTS.md when present, so shared workflow policy remains in one reviewable source instead of drifting copies.

humanDecisionResolutions.question: What review/approval policy is required before writing any generated template into live ~/.claude?
humanDecisionResolutions.resolution: Default to a strict manual gate: separate issue/PR, dry-run rendered diff, dangerous-key validation, backup of any live file, and explicit human approval before any write.

liveClaudeApplyPolicyOptions.policy: Require a separate issue/PR, dry-run diff, validation pass, live-file backup, and explicit human approval before writing any ~/.claude or project .claude file.
Allow a local apply command only after the dry-run checker passes and creates a backup; still forbid credential, auth, MCP, env-secret, hook, and default-model changes.
Never write live Claude config from the harness; render instructions and let the human manually copy reviewed entries.
